title: Free Demo — 10 Sample Plumber Questions
description: Try 10 questions from across all 306A exam topics. No signup required.

questions.question: According to the NPC, drainage pipes that are NPS 3 or less shall have a minimum downward slope of:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.8.1.(1), drainage pipes NPS 3 or less shall have a downward slope in the direction of flow of at least 1 in 50. This equals approximately 1/4 inch per foot or 2%.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an acceptable method to prevent back-siphonage according to the NPC?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.2.2.(2), back-siphonage shall be prevented by an air gap, atmospheric vacuum breaker, pressure vacuum breaker, or spill-resistant pressure vacuum breaker. A check valve alone is NOT acceptable for back-siphonage prevention.

questions.question: According to Table 2.4.9.3 of the NPC, what is the hydraulic load (in fixture units) for a water closet with a flush tank?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.9.3, a water closet with a flush tank has a hydraulic load of 4 fixture units and requires a minimum NPS 3 fixture outlet pipe. A water closet with a direct flush valve has a higher load of 6 fixture units.

questions.question: When conducting an air pressure test on a drainage or venting system, what pressure must be created and for how long?
options: A) 35 kPa for at least 15 minutes
B) 70 kPa for at least 10 minutes
C) 50 kPa for at least 30 minutes
D) 100 kPa for at least 5 minutes
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.6.5.(1), air shall be forced into the system until a pressure of 35 kPa is created, and this pressure shall be maintained for at least 15 minutes without a drop in pressure.

questions.question: The temperature-sensing element of a temperature-relief valve on a storage-type service water heater must be located within what distance from the top of the tank?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.1.7.(2)(a), the temperature-sensing element must be located within the top 150 mm of the tank. The valve must be designed to keep water temperature from exceeding 99°C.

questions.question: A vent pipe terminal in outside air must be located not less than what distance above and from every air inlet, openable window, or door?
options: A) Not less than 500 mm above and 2 m in any direction
B) Not less than 1 m above and 3.5 m in any other direction
C) Not less than 2 m above and 5 m in any other direction
D) Not less than 150 mm above and 1 m in any other direction
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.6.5.(4)(a), where a vent pipe is terminated in outside air, the terminal shall be located not less than 1 m above and not less than 3.5 m in any other direction from every air inlet, openable window or door.

questions.question: According to the NPC, the minimum nominal pipe size of a fixture outlet pipe for a water closet is:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.9.3, a water closet (both flush tank and direct flush valve types) requires a minimum fixture outlet pipe size of NPS 3.

questions.question: According to the NPC, the maximum developed length of a fixture outlet pipe shall not exceed:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.8.2.(1), the developed length of fixture outlet pipes shall not exceed 1 200 mm, except for fixture outlet pipes installed in conformance with island plumbing fixture installations.

questions.question: At what minimum size must a cleanout be provided where a building drain leaves the building?
options: D) The same size as the building drain
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.7.1.(6), building drains shall be provided with a cleanout fitting of NPS 4 or larger that is located as close as practical to the place where the building drain leaves the building.

questions.question: A water pressure test on a drainage system consists of applying a water column of at least:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.6.4.(1), a water pressure test shall consist in applying a water column of at least 3 m to all joints. The system or section shall be kept filled with water for 15 minutes.
